holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: INTC at $132.94 is up nearly 10% on the week, which is a striking move for a name that historically traded as a turnaround story — but there are no dated company catalysts in front of us right now (no confirmed earnings date, no ex-div), so there's no specific window to lean into. The only thing on the calendar is triple witching ~85 days out, which is a flow/volatility event, not a fundamental one. Without a dated print to anchor a trim or add, the honest move is to let the tape define the next entry rather than chase a stretched 5-day.
INTC.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro flow event, not company-specific)
INTC.bull: Momentum has clearly turned, +9.8% in 5 days suggests a real bid is rebuilding the story.
INTC.bear: No dated company catalyst to support the move; stretched tape can mean-revert hard on any sector wobble.

NVDA.read: No dated company catalyst is on the tape for NVDA right now — next earnings isn't even on the calendar provided — and the stock is bleeding modestly (-1.5% day, -4.3% week) without a binary event to lean on. With only a far-out triple witching as a market-structure marker, there's no edge in forcing action here; the window to add or trim opens when an earnings date is confirmed or guidance/AI-capex headlines re-rate the name. Let the tape define the next level before committing.
NVDA.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no NVDA-specific dated catalyst on deck)
NVDA.bull: AI-capex cycle still the dominant earnings driver; pullbacks into quality have been buyable.
NVDA.bear: Stretched ownership and no near-term catalyst means drift lower can continue on any AI-spend wobble.

ORCL.read: ORCL is down 16.8% over five sessions with another 3.1% down today, so the tape is doing the work — but with no dated company catalyst on the board, there's nothing forcing a decision here. The AI/OCI capex narrative that drove the run is being repriced, and without an earnings date or analyst day to anchor a recovery window, adds should wait for either a confirmed print date or a clear momentum stabilization. Triple witching on 9/18 is calendar noise, not a thesis catalyst.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no confirmed ORCL-specific event on deck)
ORCL.bull: OCI backlog and RPO growth still intact; a 17% drawdown resets sentiment and rebuilds the setup into the next print.
ORCL.bear: No near-term catalyst to break the downtrend; if AI-capex monetization questions persist, the slide can extend before any company event resets the narrative.

NOK.read: NOK is grinding higher quietly (+1.1% on the day and over the week) with no dated company catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div in the window provided. With nothing binary in sight and the only calendar item being triple witching ~85 days out, there's no real event-driven edge to lean on here; this is a hold-and-collect tape until a Q3 print date or dividend ex-date gets pinned down.
NOK.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no NOK-specific catalyst on calendar)
NOK.bull: Quiet uptrend with constructive tape and AI/networking capex tailwinds; no near-term print risk to disturb the drift.
NOK.bear: No dated catalyst means no forced re-rating; position can stall, and any surprise pre-announcement or FX headline hits without warning.

NBIS.read: No dated company catalyst on the tape for NBIS — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div, and the position is a 1-share tracker, so there's no real risk to manage here. The only thing on the calendar is Sep 18 triple witching ~85 days out, which is a flow event, not an NBIS-specific catalyst. Until an earnings date or AI-capacity/customer announcement gets dated, there's no window to lean into either direction.
NBIS.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro flow event, not NBIS-specific)
NBIS.bull: AI infra demand keeps GPU-cloud names like NBIS bid; any dated hyperscaler/customer deal or capacity update reopens the momentum window.
NBIS.bear: High-beta AI compute name with no near-term dated catalyst means it trades on sentiment and positioning — vulnerable to any AI capex wobble or witching-week derisking.

KO.read: Tiny 5-share KO position is flat-to-slightly-green on the week (+0.64%) with no dated company catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div in the feed. With nothing binary in front of it and only a distant triple-witching date 85 days out, there's no real window to lean into here; this is a quiet sit-and-collect name until an earnings date or ex-div gets confirmed.
KO.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no KO-specific dated event on the board)
KO.bull: Defensive staple, steady dividend, low-beta ballast that doesn't need a catalyst to do its job.
KO.bear: No near-term catalyst means no edge — opportunity cost vs. names with actual setups, and a broad-tape risk-off into Sept witching could drag it sideways.

SPCX.read: SPCX is a brand-new listing (June 2026 IPO) with no company-confirmed earnings or dividend dates yet in coverage, so there's no dated catalyst to anchor an add/trim window against. The first real event will be the maiden quarterly print as a public company, which typically sets the post-IPO trading regime — until then, lockup expiry chatter and the Sep 18 triple witching are the only timeline markers worth tracking. Position is small (10 shares), so the right posture is to let the first earnings calendar drop define the window rather than force a stance now.
SPCX.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (only dated event on the tape; first SPCX earnings date not yet published)
SPCX.bull: Scarcity premium in a marquee new listing with no confirmed earnings date keeps a supply-constrained float bid into year-end.
SPCX.bear: First post-IPO print and any lockup unlock are undated landmines that can reset the multiple violently in either direction.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
